title: POV: Rush Home to Aquapark
hook_3s: POV: the second school ends, I sprint home to hit the slides.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Live-action POV — backpack bouncing, hand shoves open the front door, sneakers skid down the hallway.
3-6s: POV flops face-first onto the bed, phone whips up to camera, thumb taps the aquapark.io icon.
6-11s: Hard cut into gameplay — character launches down a bright blue slide, steers hard, BUMPS two opponents off the edge into the void.
11-14s: Hang-glider section, overtakes the leader at the finish line, confetti + #1 on the leaderboard.
14-15s: App icon + orange Install button pulses on screen.
visual_direction: Open with handheld, sun-drenched live-action (warm domestic tones) to set the 'finally home' satisfaction beat, then smash-cut into aquapark.io's signature 3D low-poly world: cyan #42bcf5 water, glossy white #ffffff slide rails, and punchy orange #ff8c1c HUD/CTA accents. Keep the gameplay portion authentic — same chunky animal avatar, inner tube, slide chicane, and progress bar as the real app. Energetic, competitive, cartoonish; no filters that mute the palette.
rationale: Voodoo's current aquapark.io rotation is 100% in-game gameplay voiceover ("slide, jump, push opponents") — they have zero live-action UGC/POV hooks, despite POV "rush home to play" being a proven satisfaction archetype trending in the scan. This brief grafts that underrepresented UGC framing onto authentic aquapark gameplay, adding a relatable Gen-Z entry point before the bump-off payoff. Run as an incremental top-of-funnel test against the existing gameplay-only creatives to capture audiences who scroll past polished gameplay ads.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic aquapark.io in-game screenshot style: 3D low-poly bright colorful cartoonish chunky animal avatar riding an inflatable inner tube down a glossy white (#ffffff) curved water slide over bright cyan (#42bcf5) water, two rival avatars getting bumped off the slide edge mid-air with splash particles, vibrant orange (#ff8c1c) HUD with race progress bar at top and power-up icon at bottom-right, motion blur on the slide rails, on-screen text overlay 'BUMP 'EM OFF 💦' in bold rounded white font with orange outline, sunny sky backdrop, same UI chrome as aquapark.io.

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, live-action POV first-person shot of a teen's hand holding a phone above their face while lying on a bed, phone screen clearly displays the aquapark.io app icon and a launching 3D low-poly water slide scene with cyan (#42bcf5) water, white (#ffffff) slides, and orange (#ff8c1c) play button, warm bedroom lighting, on-screen text overlay 'opening aquapark.io' in bold white sans-serif with orange drop shadow, palette accents #42bcf5 #ffffff #ff8c1c, photoreal UGC look transitioning into cartoonish game art.

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, aquapark.io endgame moment: 3D low-poly cartoonish character on a bright orange (#ff8c1c) hang glider soaring past the finish banner above cyan (#42bcf5) water, white (#ffffff) confetti bursts, leaderboard UI in corner showing '1st' highlighted in orange, race progress bar full, bright colorful low-poly style, on-screen text overlay '#1 — gg' in chunky white font with orange stroke, authentic aquapark.io HUD chrome, no extra invented characters.
